entries.summary: Students at Dhaka University began protests demanding end to job quota system.
Protests turned violent after attacks by ruling party youth wing on demonstrators.
Video showed protesters being ambushed by gunfire at Dhaka University.
Authorities cut internet nationwide in attempt to suppress protests.
Nationwide curfew imposed; armed forces recorded firing on crowds of protesters.
Supreme Court scaled back government job quota to five percent.
Partial internet connectivity restored; protest leaders briefly paused demands.
Protesters renewed demands including release of leaders and reopening universities.
Protests resumed with calls for Sheikh Hasina to step down.
Nearly 100 people killed during protests; total toll reached around 300.
Hasina resigned and fled to India after protesters stormed her official residence.
Bangladesh's president ordered release of opposition leader Khaleda Zia.
Bangladesh moved to seek former PM Hasina's extradition from India.
Yunus said elections would follow reforms, declining to set a date.
Former PM Khaleda Zia returned to Bangladesh ahead of expected elections.
Interim leader Muhammad Yunus signalled he was considering resignation.
Leaked recording revealed Hasina ordering security forces to fire on 2024 protesters.
Bangladesh marked one year since Hasina's fall amid unresolved reform debates.
Awami League supporters protested across Bangladesh over crackdown and ongoing trial.
International Crimes Tribunal convicted Hasina in absentia and sentenced her to death.
Killing of a 2024 uprising activist triggered renewed unrest across Bangladesh.
Students at Dhaka University began protests over civil service job quotas.
Demonstrations turned violent amid escalating government response.
At least 91 people killed in single day of clashes between police and protesters.
Hasina resigned and fled to India by helicopter after weeks of unrest.
Crowds stormed Hasina's official residence and looted its contents.
Bangabandhu Memorial Museum and Awami League offices were torched in Dhaka.
Bangladesh president dissolved parliament and ordered Khaleda Zia's release.
Muhammad Yunus named to lead interim government at student protesters' request.
Students protested near vandalised Hasina mural demanding accountability.
Bangladesh court issued arrest warrant for former prime minister Hasina.
Yunus said elections could be held by end of 2025.
Thousands rallied at March for Unity in Dhaka marking the uprising.
Activists stormed and demolished founding leader Mujibur Rahman's historic Dhaka home.
Bangladesh summoned an Indian diplomat over Hasina's comments from exile.
International Crimes Tribunal convicted Hasina in absentia and imposed death sentence.
Bangladesh's foreign ministry demanded India hand Hasina over without delay.
